content: Physical activity promotion strategies: (1) Individual counseling—exercise prescription using FITT principle (Frequency, Intensity, Time, Type), motivational interviewing, behavioral goal-setting, self-monitoring tools; (2) Clinical programs—cardiac rehabilitation, pulmonary rehabilitation, diabetes prevention program (DPP showing 58% diabetes risk reduction with 150 min/week activity); (3) Community interventions—walking groups, park-based programs, workplace wellness challenges, active transport initiatives; (4) Environmental/policy approaches—complete streets design, safe routes to school, park/recreation facility investment, building codes for stairs, point-of-decision prompts; (5) Digital interventions—wearable activity trackers, smartphone apps, exergaming. Evidence supports multi-component interventions. Barriers addressed through accessible facilities, culturally appropriate programs, childcare provision, and addressing safety concerns. Any activity better than none; incremental increases beneficial.
